title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance AI assistants working shinymcp codebase.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: shinymcp R package converts Shiny apps MCP (Model Context Protocol) Apps. MCP Apps render inside AI chat interfaces (Claude, ChatGPT, VS Code) using ui:// resources, sandboxed iframes, postMessage/JSON-RPC communication. Key capabilities: - Parse → Analyze → Generate pipeline automatic Shiny--MCP conversion - MCP-compatible UI components (mcp_select(), mcp_text_input(), mcp_plot(), etc.) - Self-contained JS bridge MCP Apps postMessage/JSON-RPC protocol - McpApp R6 runtime class bundling UI + tools - Resource protocol handler ui:// resources - MCP server supporting stdio HTTP transports - Deputy skill AI-assisted conversion complex apps

title: CLAUDE.md
code: shinymcp/ ├── R/ │   ├── shinymcp-package.R     # Package-level docs │   ├── parse.R                # Shiny app AST parser → ShinyAppIR │   ├── analyze.R              # Reactive graph → tool group clusters │   ├── generate.R             # Code generator (HTML + tools.R + server.R) │   ├── convert.R              # Top-level convert_app() entry point │   ├── mcp-app.R              # McpApp R6 class (runtime) │   ├── components-input.R     # mcp_select(), mcp_text_input(), mcp_input(), mcp_output() │   ├── components-output.R    # mcp_plot(), mcp_text(), mcp_table() │   ├── js-bridge.R            # JS bridge config generation + injection │   ├── serve.R                # MCP server with tools + ui:// resources │   ├── mcp-resources.R        # Resource protocol handler │   ├── utils.R                # Internal utilities │   └── errors.R               # Custom error classes ├── inst/ │   ├── js/shinymcp-bridge.js  # MCP Apps JS bridge (~300 lines) │   ├── templates/app.html     # HTML skeleton template │   ├── skills/                # Deputy skill for AI conversion │   └── examples/              # Example MCP Apps ├── tests/testthat/            # Unit tests (testthat edition 3) └── man/                       # Auto-generated roxygen2 docs

title: CLAUDE.md
CLAUDE.md
text: Formatter: Air (config air.toml) Documentation: roxygen2 markdown support Classes: R6 McpApp, ResourceRegistry Errors: Custom error classes via rlang::abort() (see R/errors.R) Style: tidyverse conventions, %||% operator rlang

title: CLAUDE.md
text: JS bridge auto-detects inputs matching tool argument names DOM element id attributes. means native shiny::selectInput(), shiny::numericInput(), etc. work without wrappers — long element’s id matches tool argument name. Resolution priority (resolveInputElement()): 1. Explicit data-shinymcp-input="{id}" attribute (backward compat, mcp_select() etc.) 2. Standard form elements id: select#id, input#id, textarea#id, button#id 3. Container id holding radio inputs (radio group pattern) mcp_*() input components (mcp_select(), mcp_text_input(), etc.) still work used conversion pipeline. mcp_input(tag) mcp_output(tag) escape hatches stamping data-shinymcp-* attributes arbitrary tags.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Components generate htmltools tags data-shinymcp-* attributes: - Inputs: data-shinymcp-input="{id}", data-shinymcp-type="{type}" - Outputs: data-shinymcp-output="{id}", data-shinymcp-output-type="{type}"

title: CLAUDE.md
text: parse_shiny_app(path) → ShinyAppIR (AST walking) analyze_reactive_graph(ir) → ReactiveAnalysis (dependency graph) generate_mcp_app(analysis, ir, output_dir) → files disk convert_app(path) → orchestrates full pipeline

title: CLAUDE.md
text: MCP Apps use: - ui:// resource URIs declare HTML content - text/html;profile=mcp-app MIME type - postMessage/JSON-RPC host ↔︎ iframe communication - Tool annotations _meta.ui.resourceUri link tools UI

title: CLAUDE.md
text: self-contained bridge (inst/js/shinymcp-bridge.js): - Reads config (including toolArgs) <script id="shinymcp-config"> element - Builds input cache matching tool arg names DOM elements (buildInputCache()) - Listens input changes → sends ui/update-model-context debounced tools/call host - Receives ui/tool-result → updates DOM output elements via structuredContent keys - Reports size changes via ResizeObserver - Handles teardown cleanup - ES5-compatible (const/let/arrow functions/Set), includes CSS.escape polyfill

title: CLAUDE.md
text: htmltools, Shiny runtime - dependency Shiny’s JS. JS bridge replaces shiny.js entirely. Auto-detect inputs - bridge matches tool argument names DOM element ids, native shiny/bslib inputs work without wrappers. mcp_*() components data-shinymcp-input attributes remain explicit overrides. One tool per reactive group - Connected inputs/reactives/outputs map single tool. Self-contained JS - npm build step. Vanilla ES5 JS IIFE. mcptools extension path - Resource handling mcp-resources.R designed eventual upstream PR.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Core (Imports): R6, htmltools, jsonlite, cli, rlang Optional (Suggests): ellmer, mcptools, shiny, base64enc, httpuv, testthat, deputy, knitr

text: project uses bd (beads) issue tracking. Run bd onboard get started.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: bd ready              # Find available work bd show <id>          # View issue details bd update <id> --status in_progress  # Claim work bd close <id>         # Complete work bd sync               # Sync with git

title: CLAUDE.md
text: ending work session, complete steps . Work complete git push succeeds. File issues remaining work - Create issues anything needs follow-Run quality gates (code changed) - Tests, linters, builds Update issue status - Close finished work, update -progress items Push remote: Clean - Clear stashes, prune remote branches Verify - changes committed pushed Hand - Provide context next session
code: git pull --rebase bd sync git push git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"

text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement github@jameshwade.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: Point convert_app() Shiny app directory: generated directory contains:
code: library(shinymcp)  convert_app("path/to/my-shiny-app") ── Converting Shiny app to MCP App ──────────────────── Source: path/to/my-shiny-app Output: path/to/my-shiny-app_mcp  ── Parsing ℹ Found 4 input(s) and 2 output(s) ℹ Complexity: medium  ── Analyzing ℹ Identified 1 tool group(s)  ── Generating ✔ Generated MCP App in path/to/my-shiny-app_mcp

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: convert_app() runs three stages sequence: can run stage independently finer control.

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: parse_shiny_app() reads app’s R source extracts structured intermediate representation (IR): parser handles app.R (single-file) ui.R/server.R (split-file) apps. walks AST extract: Inputs: *Input() calls IDs, types, labels, arguments Outputs: *Output() calls IDs types Server body: body server function Reactives: reactive() expressions input dependencies Observers: observe() observeEvent() calls Input refs: Every input$name input[["name"]] reference parser classifies apps complexity:
code: ir <- parse_shiny_app("path/to/my-shiny-app") ir ── Shiny App IR ─────────────────────────────────────── Path: path/to/my-shiny-app Inputs: 4 Outputs: 2 Reactives: 1 Observers: 0 Complexity: medium Input refs: species, x_var, y_var, trend

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: analyze_reactive_graph() takes IR builds dependency graph: analyzer: Builds dependency graph — nodes inputs, reactives, outputs; edges represent data flow (input$x used reactive(...) used renderPlot(...)) Finds connected components — uses union-find group nodes transitively connected Maps components tool groups — connected component becomes one tool component’s inputs arguments outputs return values Flags unresolvable patterns — dynamic UI, file uploads, download handlers, observers side effects example, Shiny app reactive() feeds renderPlot() renderText() produces single tool group containing related inputs outputs.
code: analysis <- analyze_reactive_graph(ir) analysis ── Reactive Analysis ────────────────────────────────── Nodes: 7 Edges: 6 Tool groups: 1

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: generate_mcp_app() writes MCP App files: generates: UI code using shinymcp components (mcp_select(), mcp_plot(), etc.) mapped original Shiny inputs outputs Tool definitions using ellmer::tool() proper argument types (type_string, type_number, type_boolean) tool annotations Server entrypoint sources tools sets state environment Conversion notes (complex apps) listing needs manual review

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: pipeline handles Shiny patterns box:

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: generated tools contain placeholder function bodies. need copy actual computation logic original render*() functions generated tool functions. simple apps, straightforward — tool arguments match original inputs return structure matches outputs. complex apps, review areas:

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: generator creates tool skeleton uses placeholder code like paste("Result :", x). Replace actual computation:
code: # Generated placeholder: update_scatter_and_stats <- ellmer::tool(   fun = function(species, x_var, y_var, trend) {     # TODO: Insert computation logic from original render function here     paste("Result for:", species, x_var, y_var, trend)   },   # ... )  # After manual review — fill in the real logic: update_scatter_and_stats <- ellmer::tool(   fun = function(species = "All", x_var = "bill_length_mm",                  y_var = "bill_depth_mm", trend = FALSE) {     data <- palmerpenguins::penguins[complete.cases(palmerpenguins::penguins), ]     if (species != "All") data <- data[data$species == species, ]      p <- ggplot2::ggplot(data, ggplot2::aes(.data[[x_var]], .data[[y_var]])) +       ggplot2::geom_point()     if (isTRUE(trend)) p <- p + ggplot2::geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)      tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")     ggplot2::ggsave(tmp, p, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 144, bg = "white")     on.exit(unlink(tmp))      list(       scatter = base64enc::base64encode(tmp),       stats = paste(capture.output(summary(data)), collapse = "\n")     )   },   # ... )

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: uiOutput() / renderUI() flagged warning. Replace mcp_html() return HTML strings tool:
code:
# Instead of renderUI(), return HTML from the tool: fun = function(show_detail = FALSE) { html <- if (show_detail) { "

Details

More info here

" } else { "" } list(detail_panel = html) }

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: observe() observeEvent() calls flagged since often perform side effects (database writes, file operations) don’t map cleanly stateless tool calls. Review observer decide: Can folded tool’s return value? need write file system? (Use temp files) Can removed entirely?

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: fileInput() supported. generator warns . Use mcp_text_input() file paths instead, tool read known directory.

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: default, convert_app() writes {path}_mcp/. Specify custom output directory:

title: Automatic Shiny App Conversion
text: ’ve filled tool bodies: Test locally serve(app) verify UI renders tools respond Register Claude Desktop adding app config Iterate — adjust layouts bslib, tweak tool descriptions better AI interactions detailed walkthrough building MCP App scratch (without automatic pipeline), see vignette("converting-shiny-apps").

title: Choose the right migration path
text: Use know compact interaction want. path gives cleanest runtime behavior preview, shinychat, MCP hosts.
code: app <- mcp_app(   ui = htmltools::tagList(     mcp_select("dataset", "Dataset", c("mtcars", "iris")),     mcp_text("summary")   ),   tools = list(     list(       name = "inspect_dataset",       description = "Inspect a built-in dataset",       inputSchema = list(         type = "object",         properties = list(dataset = list(type = "string"))       ),       fun = function(dataset = "mtcars") {         list(summary = paste(capture.output(summary(get(dataset))), collapse = "\n"))       }     )   ) )

title: Choose the right migration path
text: Use already bounded module server-side computation trust. Keep Shiny UI fragment. Expose inputs outputs want card. Supply explicit tool handler instead expecting automatic execution arbitrary server code.

title: Choose the right migration path
text: Use as_mcp_apps() convert_app(mode = "cards") original Shiny app contains several connected reactive islands become separate chat cards. path still scaffold output: tool bodies remain placeholders large reactive groups still need review side effects unsupported widgets still need manual treatment

title: Choose the right migration path
text: Use convert_app(mode = "scaffold") want starting point manual completion. Always review CONVERSION_NOTES.md. generated code claim source app now bounded headless runtime.

title: Choose the right migration path
text: Use rule thumb: authored handlers , runtime path. convert_app() generated handlers, assume scaffold output replace placeholders.

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: converting, helps understand changes: core shift: flatten reactive graph tool functions. connected group inputs → reactives → outputs becomes single tool accepts inputs arguments returns outputs named list.

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: Start Shiny app list every input output. ’s typical penguins Shiny app: app, identify: Inputs: species — selectInput 4 choices x_var — selectInput 4 variable choices y_var — selectInput 4 variable choices trend — checkboxInput (boolean) Outputs: scatter — plotOutput (ggplot scatter plot) stats — verbatimTextOutput (summary statistics) Reactive logic: filtered_data() filters penguins species outputs depend filtered_data() plus axis/trend inputs Everything connected — one reactive group → one tool
code: # --- Original Shiny app --- library(shiny) library(ggplot2) library(palmerpenguins)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Palmer Penguins Explorer"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       selectInput("species", "Species", c("All", "Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo")),       selectInput("x_var", "X axis", c("bill_length_mm", "bill_depth_mm",                                         "flipper_length_mm", "body_mass_g")),       selectInput("y_var", "Y axis", c("bill_depth_mm", "bill_length_mm",                                         "flipper_length_mm", "body_mass_g")),       checkboxInput("trend", "Show trend line", FALSE)     ),     mainPanel(       plotOutput("scatter"),       verbatimTextOutput("stats")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   filtered_data <- reactive({     data <- penguins[complete.cases(penguins), ]     if (input$species != "All") {       data <- data[data$species == input$species, ]     }     data   })    output$scatter <- renderPlot({     p <- ggplot(filtered_data(),                 aes(x = .data[[input$x_var]], y = .data[[input$y_var]],                     color = species)) +       geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2.5)     if (input$trend) p <- p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)     p   })    output$stats <- renderPrint({     summary(filtered_data()[, c(input$x_var, input$y_var, "species")])   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: JS bridge auto-detects inputs matching tool argument names element id attributes. means can keep Shiny inputs -— just ensure id matches tool argument name: outputs, replace Shiny outputs shinymcp equivalents: IDs stay . need mark non-standard element output, use mcp_output(tag, id, type).

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: Use bslib layout instead Shiny’s fluidPage() / sidebarLayout(). bslib components work directly htmltools — Shiny server needed. Standard shiny::selectInput(), shiny::checkboxInput(), etc. auto-detected bridge: Named choice vectors (e.g. "Bill Length (mm)" = "bill_length_mm") work exactly like Shiny — name displayed, value sent tool.
code: library(shinymcp) library(bslib) library(htmltools)  ui <- page_sidebar(   theme = bs_theme(preset = "shiny"),   title = "Palmer Penguins Explorer",   sidebar = sidebar(     width = 260,     shiny::selectInput(       "species", "Species",       c("All", "Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo")     ),     shiny::selectInput("x_var", "X axis", c(       "Bill Length (mm)" = "bill_length_mm",       "Bill Depth (mm)" = "bill_depth_mm",       "Flipper Length (mm)" = "flipper_length_mm",       "Body Mass (g)" = "body_mass_g"     )),     shiny::selectInput("y_var", "Y axis", c(       "Bill Depth (mm)" = "bill_depth_mm",       "Bill Length (mm)" = "bill_length_mm",       "Flipper Length (mm)" = "flipper_length_mm",       "Body Mass (g)" = "body_mass_g"     )),     shiny::checkboxInput("trend", "Show trend line")   ),   card(     card_header("Scatter Plot"),     mcp_plot("scatter", height = "380px")   ),   card(     card_header("Summary Statistics"),     mcp_text("stats")   ) )

what: Step 4: Convert reactive logic to a tool
title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text:
key step. Flatten reactive graph single function: function arguments input values (sensible defaults) function body reactives renderers function returns named list mapping output IDs values plots, render temporary PNG return base64-encoded image. bridge displays element. Important details: Return keys match output IDs: list(scatter = ..., stats = ...) corresponds mcp_plot("scatter") mcp_text("stats"). bridge uses keys route values correct output elements. Base64 plots: Use ggsave() png() + dev.(), base64enc::base64encode(). bridge wraps tag. Text output: mcp_text() renders 
 tag, R’s summary() cat() output preserves column alignment. Default arguments: Every argument needs default value. tool uses loaded initially AI sees expected format. Stateless: reactiveVal(), <<-, session state. tool call recomputes scratch. apps fine since tool calls fast.
code:
tools <- list(   ellmer::tool(     fun = function(       species = "All",       x_var = "bill_length_mm",       y_var = "bill_depth_mm",       trend = FALSE     ) {       data <- palmerpenguins::penguins       data <- data[complete.cases(data), ]        # Filter (was: filtered_data reactive)       if (species != "All") {         data <- data[data$species == species, ]       }        # Plot (was: renderPlot)       p <- ggplot2::ggplot(         data,         ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = .data[[y_var]], color = species)       ) +         ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2.5) +         ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 13)        if (isTRUE(trend)) {         p <- p + ggplot2::geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)       }        # Render plot to base64 PNG       tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")       ggplot2::ggsave(tmp, p, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 144, bg = "white")       on.exit(unlink(tmp))       plot_b64 <- base64enc::base64encode(tmp)        # Summary text (was: renderPrint)       stats <- paste(capture.output({         cat(sprintf("Observations: %d penguins

", nrow(data)))         print(summary(data[, c(x_var, y_var, "species")]))       }), collapse = "
")        # Return named list: keys must match output IDs       list(scatter = plot_b64, stats = stats)     },     name = "explore_penguins",     description = "Explore the Palmer Penguins dataset with scatter plots",     arguments = list(       species = ellmer::type_string("Species filter: All, Adelie, Chinstrap, or Gentoo"),       x_var = ellmer::type_string("X axis variable name"),       y_var = ellmer::type_string("Y axis variable name"),       trend = ellmer::type_boolean("Whether to show a linear trend line")     )   ) )

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: Wire UI, tools, server: Save single app.R file. register Claude Desktop, add entry Claude Desktop config (~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json macOS): Restart Claude Desktop, app appear interactive UI explore_penguins tool called.

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: outputs share reactive dependencies, belong single tool. common case:
code: # Shiny: two outputs from one reactive server <- function(input, output, session) {   data <- reactive({ mtcars[mtcars$cyl == input$cyl, ] })   output$plot <- renderPlot({ plot(data()$mpg) })   output$text <- renderText({ paste(nrow(data()), "cars") }) }  # MCP App: one tool returning both ellmer::tool(   fun = function(cyl = 4) {     data <- mtcars[mtcars$cyl == cyl, ]     list(       plot = render_plot_base64(function() plot(data$mpg)),       text = paste(nrow(data), "cars")     )   },   # ... )

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: outputs completely independent inputs, use separate tools. bridge calls tools whose inputs changed:
code: # Tool 1: only uses the "dataset" input ellmer::tool(   fun = function(dataset = "mtcars") { ... },   name = "summarize_data",   # ... )  # Tool 2: only uses the "n" input ellmer::tool(   fun = function(n = 100) { ... },   name = "generate_sample",   # ... )

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: MCP tool calls stateless. Shiny app uses reactiveVal() reactiveValues() accumulate state across interactions, two options: Recompute inputs: filtering/selection state can derived current input values alone. Use file system: truly stateful apps (e.g., todo list), write state temp file read back next tool call.

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: tableOutput, return HTML table string. knitr::kable() easiest approach: Use mcp_table("my_table") UI. bridge sets innerHTML directly.
code: fun = function(dataset = "mtcars") {   data <- head(get(dataset, envir = asNamespace("datasets")), 10)   list(     my_table = knitr::kable(data, format = "html")   ) }

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: MCP Apps don’t support renderUI() / uiOutput(). Instead: Use mcp_html(id) return HTML strings tool show/hide, return empty string element hidden dynamic choices, use fixed mcp_select() document valid values tool description

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: Since MCP Apps use htmltools directly (Shiny’s fluidPage), use bslib layout. Common patterns: CSS JS dependencies bslib automatically inlined MCP App’s HTML resource.
code: # Sidebar layout (standard shiny inputs auto-detected) page_sidebar(   sidebar = sidebar(     shiny::selectInput("x", "Variable", choices),     shiny::checkboxInput("log", "Log scale")   ),   card(mcp_plot("main_plot")),   card(mcp_text("summary")) )  # Multi-column layout page(   theme = bs_theme(preset = "shiny"),   layout_columns(     col_widths = c(6, 6),     card(card_header("Plot"), mcp_plot("plot1")),     card(card_header("Table"), mcp_table("table1"))   ) )  # Stacked cards page(   card(card_header("Controls"), shiny::selectInput("x", "Pick", c("a", "b"))),   card(card_header("Output"), mcp_text("result")) )

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: Shiny features don’t MCP App equivalents: fileInput: MCP Apps can’t receive file uploads. Use mcp_text_input() file paths instead, tool read known directory. Shiny modules internal state: Flatten module logic top-level tools. invalidateLater / polling: MCP tools request-response . background updates. JavaScript-heavy widgets (DT, plotly, leaflet): require Shiny’s JS runtime. Use static alternatives (base R plots, knitr::kable tables).

title: Converting Shiny Apps to MCP Apps
text: complete converted penguins app available :

title: Debugging shinymcp
text: Start parser analyzer: tells whether shinymcp found reactive islands expected.
code: ir <- parse_shiny_app("path/to/app") analysis <- analyze_reactive_graph(ir)  analysis$tool_groups ir$inputs ir$outputs

title: Debugging shinymcp
text: preview_app() now uses shared host controller exposes protocol log preview page. Use inspect: ui/initialize tools/call ui/update-model-context ui/notifications/size-changed

title: Debugging shinymcp
text: Use format_tool_result() development want see exactly runtime send back host: shinychat wrappers, inspect ContentToolResult directly:
code: format_tool_result(list(   summary = mcp_result_text("ready"),   table = mcp_result_table(head(mtcars)) )) tool <- as_shinychat_tool(card_app) result <- tool(dataset = "mtcars")  result@value result@extra$display

title: Debugging shinymcp
text: Input ids match tool argument names. Large converted groups really split smaller cards. Generated conversion tools still placeholders. Rich display works shinychat, plain fallback text still matters hosts.

title: Use shinymcp with shinychat
text: wrapped tool returns: machine-facing value, defaults raw tool result transformed value_fn. human-facing shinychat tool card, defaults live embedded McpApp.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(ellmer) library(shinychat) library(shinymcp)  card_app <- mcp_app(   ui = htmltools::tagList(     mcp_text_input("name", "Name"),     mcp_text("greeting")   ),   tools = list(     list(       name = "greet",       description = "Generate a greeting card",       inputSchema = list(         type = "object",         properties = list(name = list(type = "string"))       ),       fun = function(name = "world") {         list(greeting = paste("Hello", name))       }     )   ),   name = "greeting-card" )  greet_tool <- as_shinychat_tool(   card_app,   value_fn = function(raw_result) list(greeting = raw_result$greeting),   summary = function(raw_result) raw_result$greeting,   title = "Greeting Card" )  ui <- page_fillable(chat_mod_ui("chat"))  server <- function(input, output, session) {   client <- ellmer::chat("openai/gpt-4.1-nano")   client$register_tool(greet_tool)   chat_mod_server("chat", client) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Use shinymcp with shinychat
text: Use mcp_content_result() already live Shiny session want append card :
code: ui <- page_fillable(chat_ui("chat"))  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     card <- mcp_content_result(       app = card_app,       value = list(status = "ready"),       title = "Greeting Card",       text = "Adjust the card inputs to refine the result."     )      chat_append("chat", card)   }) }

title: Use shinymcp with shinychat
text: authored Shiny UIs, use host shell helpers directly: mcp_embed(card_app) convenience helper dynamic server-side contexts live session already exists.
code: ui <- page_fillable(   mcp_host_ui("card") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   host <- mcp_host_server(     "card",     app = card_app,     trigger = "submit"   ) }

title: Use shinymcp with shinychat
text: mcp_embed() mcp_host_server() support four trigger modes: change: execute immediately input change. debounce: execute short quiet period. submit: require host shell Apply button. manual: require explicit host-side command host$execute().

title: Use shinymcp with shinychat
text: Prefer small single-purpose cards. Prefer sequential tool execution shinychat strong reason expose many parallel cards. Treat chat card human surface value_fn output model-facing value.

what: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: shinymcp converts Shiny apps MCP Apps — interactive UIs render directly inside AI chat interfaces like Claude Desktop.

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: can install development version shinymcp GitHub :

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: MCP App two parts: UI components render chat interface, tools run R code inputs change. Use standard shiny bslib inputs — bridge auto-detects matching tool argument names element id attributes. Save app.R, register Claude Desktop config: Restart Claude Desktop. tool invoked, interactive UI appears inline conversation. Changing dropdown calls tool updates output — page reload needed.
code: library(shinymcp) library(bslib)  ui <- page_sidebar(   theme = bs_theme(preset = "shiny"),   title = "Dataset Explorer",   sidebar = sidebar(     # Standard shiny input — auto-detected because id matches tool arg "dataset"     shiny::selectInput("dataset", "Choose dataset", c("mtcars", "iris", "pressure"))   ),   card(     card_header("Summary"),     mcp_text("summary")   ) )  tools <- list(   ellmer::tool(     fun = function(dataset = "mtcars") {       data <- get(dataset, envir = asNamespace("datasets"))       paste(capture.output(summary(data)), collapse = "\n")     },     name = "get_summary",     description = "Get summary statistics for the selected dataset",     arguments = list(       dataset = ellmer::type_string("Dataset name")     )   ) )  app <- mcp_app(ui, tools, name = "dataset-explorer") serve(app) {   "mcpServers": {     "dataset-explorer": {       "command": "Rscript",       "args": ["/path/to/app.R"]     }   } }

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: core idea: flatten reactive graph tool functions. connected group inputs → reactives → outputs becomes single tool takes input values arguments returns named list outputs. Return keys (scatter, stats) must match output IDs UI (mcp_plot("scatter"), mcp_text("stats")). bridge routes value correct output element. full worked example converting Shiny app step--step, see vignette("converting-shiny-apps").
code: # --- Shiny --- server <- function(input, output, session) {   filtered <- reactive({     penguins[penguins$species == input$species, ]   })   output$scatter <- renderPlot({     ggplot(filtered(), aes(x, y)) + geom_point()   })   output$stats <- renderPrint({     summary(filtered())   }) }  # --- MCP App tool --- ellmer::tool(   fun = function(species = "Adelie") {     filtered <- penguins[penguins$species == species, ]      # Render plot to base64 PNG     tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")     ggplot2::ggsave(tmp, my_plot, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 144)     on.exit(unlink(tmp))      list(       scatter = base64enc::base64encode(tmp),       stats = paste(capture.output(summary(filtered)), collapse = "\n")     )   },   name = "explore",   description = "Filter and visualize penguins",   arguments = list(     species = ellmer::type_string("Penguin species")   ) )

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: shinymcp includes parse-analyze-generate pipeline can scaffold MCP App existing Shiny app: parses UI server code, maps reactive dependency graph tool groups, writes working MCP App tools, components, server entrypoint. generated tool bodies contain placeholders fill actual computation logic. details, see vignette("automatic-conversion").

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: complex Shiny apps, shinymcp ships deputy skill guides AI agent conversion process. skill handles dynamic UI, modules, file uploads, patterns require human judgment. See inst/skills/convert-shiny-app/SKILL.md full instructions.

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: bridge auto-detects standard form elements (<select>, <input>, etc.) whose id matches tool argument name. means can use Shiny bslib inputs already know: edge cases (id doesn’t match arg name, custom widgets), use mcp_input() explicitly mark element: shinymcp also provides lightweight mcp_select(), mcp_text_input(), etc. generate minimal HTML without Shiny’s JS runtime. useful simple apps automatic conversion pipeline generates.
code: sidebar(   shiny::selectInput("species", "Species", choices),   shiny::numericInput("n", "Count", value = 10),   shiny::checkboxInput("trend", "Show trend line") ) mcp_input(shiny::radioButtons("fmt", "Format", c("summary", "head")), id = "fmt")

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: can also turn tag output target mcp_output():

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: shinymcp ships example apps:
code: # Minimal: mcp_select() + text output system.file("examples", "hello-mcp", "app.R", package = "shinymcp")  # Native shiny/bslib inputs with auto-detection + mcp_input()/mcp_output() system.file("examples", "bslib-inputs", "app.R", package = "shinymcp")  # Full dashboard: Palmer Penguins with native shiny inputs, ggplot2, and # summary statistics system.file("examples", "penguins", "app.R", package = "shinymcp")

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: MCP Apps render inside sandboxed iframes AI chat interface. lightweight JavaScript bridge (npm dependencies) handles communication via postMessage/JSON-RPC: User changes input → bridge auto-detects form elements inputs (matching tool argument names element ids) collects values Bridge sends tools/call request host Host proxies call MCP server (R process) R tool function runs, returns results Bridge updates output elements response bridge also implements MCP Apps initialization handshake (ui/initialize), auto-resize notifications, teardown handling.

title: shinymcp - Shiny Apps Inside AI Chat
text: ellmer — LLM framework shinymcp uses tool definitions bslib — Bootstrap-based layout theming UI mcptools — MCP server framework (shinymcp extends resource support) deputy — Agentic AI framework bundled skill AI-assisted Shiny conversion

title: Convert an object to an MCP App — as_mcp_app
code: as_mcp_app(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'shiny.appobj' as_mcp_app(   x,   name = NULL,   tools = NULL,   selective = NULL,   version = "0.1.0",   ... )  # S3 method for class 'McpApp' as_mcp_app(x, ...)  # Default S3 method as_mcp_app(x, ...)

title: Convert an object to an MCP App — as_mcp_app
text: x object convert. Currently supports: shiny.appobj (shiny::shinyApp()) McpApp (returned -) character path directory containing app.R, direct path app file ... Additional arguments passed methods. name App name (used resource URIs). Defaults "shinymcp-app". tools Optional list explicit ellmer::tool() definitions. provided, used instead auto-generating tools reactive graph. selective Logical. TRUE (default bindMcp() annotations present), annotated elements exposed. FALSE, detected inputs/outputs exposed. version App version string. Defaults "0.1.0".

"scaffold" generates one scaffold app. "cards" generates compact per-group scaffold cards. selective Whether card mode split connected tool groups. max_inputs_per_card Preferred chat-card input budget. compact_layout Whether generated cards prefer compact layouts.

what: Create an MCP App from a Shiny module — mcp_tool_module
title: Create an MCP App from a Shiny module — mcp_tool_module
text: Wraps standard Shiny module (UI function + server function) McpApp. module UI rendered MCP-compatible attributes, tool definition created maps module's inputs outputs. handler provided, tool fully functional; otherwise, stub handler generated placeholder.

title: Create an MCP App from a Shiny module — mcp_tool_module
code: mcp_tool_module(   module_ui,   module_server,   name,   description,   handler = NULL,   arguments = NULL,   version = "0.1.0",   ... )

title: Create an MCP App from a Shiny module — mcp_tool_module
text: module_ui Shiny module UI function accepts id argument (e.g., function(id) { ns <- NS(id); tagList(...) }). module_server Shiny module server function. Currently stored metadata future headless Shiny session support, allow module server execute reactively tools called. name Tool/app name. Used ui:// resource URIs. description Human-readable description tool . handler Optional tool handler function. provided, function called MCP tool invoked. arguments match module's input IDs. NULL, stub handler generated. arguments Optional list ellmer::type_string(), ellmer::type_number(), etc. tool's input schema. NULL, arguments auto-detected rendered module UI. version App version string. ... Additional arguments stored module metadata (e.g., shared reactive values pass module server headless support lands).

title: Create an MCP App from a Shiny module — mcp_tool_module
text: McpApp object.

title: Create an MCP App from a Shiny module — mcp_tool_module
text: mirrors shinychat::chat_tool_module() MCP runtime — module can used contexts.

title: Create an MCP App from a Shiny module — mcp_tool_module
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(shiny)  # Define a standard Shiny module hist_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- NS(id)   tagList(     sliderInput(ns("bins"), "Bins:", min = 5, max = 50, value = 25),     plotOutput(ns("plot"), height = "250px")   ) }  hist_server <- function(id, dataset) {   moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     output$plot <- renderPlot({       hist(dataset(), breaks = input$bins, col = "#007bc2")     })   }) }  # Create and serve as MCP App app <- mcp_tool_module(   hist_ui, hist_server,   name = "histogram",   description = "Show an interactive histogram",   handler = function(bins = 25) {     tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")     grDevices::png(tmp, width = 600, height = 250)     hist(faithful$eruptions, breaks = bins, col = "#007bc2")     grDevices::dev.off()     list(plot = base64enc::base64encode(tmp))   } ) serve(app) } # }

what: Preview an MCP App in a web browser — preview_app
title: Preview an MCP App in a web browser — preview_app
text: Starts local HTTP server opens MCP App browser. lightweight host page emulates MCP Apps postMessage protocol tools fully functional — inputs trigger tool calls, outputs update real time, just like inside Claude Desktop.

title: Preview an MCP App in a web browser — preview_app
Preview an MCP App in a web browser — preview_app
text: app McpApp object, path directory containing MCP App app.R (source()d obtain app object). port Port local server. NULL (default) picks random available port. launch Whether open browser automatically (default TRUE).

title: Preview an MCP App in a web browser — preview_app
text: Invisibly, list url (preview URL) stop() (function shut server).

title: Preview an MCP App in a web browser — preview_app
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ app <- mcp_app(   ui = htmltools::tags$div(     mcp_text_input("name", "Your name"),     mcp_text("greeting")   ),   tools = list(     list(       name = "greet",       fun = function(name = "world") {         list(greeting = paste0("Hello, ", name, "!"))       }     )   ) )  # Opens in browser with working inputs/outputs srv <- preview_app(app)  # Stop when done srv$stop() } # }

what: shinymcp: Convert Shiny Apps to MCP Apps — shinymcp-package
title: shinymcp: Convert Shiny Apps to MCP Apps — shinymcp-package
text: Converts existing Shiny applications MCP (Model Context Protocol) Apps render inside AI chat interfaces. Provides parse-analyze-generate pipeline automatic conversion, MCP-compatible UI components, JavaScript bridge MCP Apps postMessage protocol, runtime framework serving MCP Apps tools ui:// resources.

title: shinymcp: Convert Shiny Apps to MCP Apps — shinymcp-package
text: Maintainer: James Wade github@jameshwade.com
